revision.text:
{{short description|Shinto kami}} [[File:Kuninotokotachi.jpg|thumb]] In [[Shinto faith]], '''Kuninotokotachi''' {{Nihongo||国之常立神||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Kami'', in ''[[Kojiki]]''|}} {{Nihongo||国常立尊||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Mikoto'', in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (no-Mikoto here being an honorific of divinity|}} or '''Kuni-toko-tachi<ref name=":12">{{Cite book |last=Evans Lansing |first=Smith |title=The Complete Idiot's Guide to World Mythology |last2=Brown |first2=Nathan Robert |publisher=[[Alpha Books]] |year=2007 |isbn=9781592577644 |location=New York |pages=14}}</ref>''' is one of the two [[kami|God]]s born from "something like a [[Reed bed|reed]] that arose from the soil"<ref>[[Masaki Tsugita]], ''Kojiki'', 1977, {{ISBN|4-06-158207-0}}</ref> when the Earth was [[Chaos (cosmogony)|chaotic]]. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, he is the first of the [[Kamiyonanayo|seven generations of Divinities]] born after the [[Kotoamatsukami|first five divinities]] were born at the time of the creation of the Universe. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}, he is the first of the three divinities born after Heaven and Earth were born out of chaos, and is born from something looking like a reed-shoot growing between heaven and earth.<ref>''Nihongi - Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 (tr. from the original Chinese and Japanese by W.G. Aston, Charles E. Tuttle Cy. 1990)''</ref> He is known by mythology to reside on top of [[Mount Fuji]] (富士山).
Kuninotokotachi is described as a [[hitorigami]] and genderless in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, but is described as a male god in {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Shoki}}.
[[Yoshida Kanetomo]], the founder of the [[Yoshida Shintō]] sect, identified Kuninotokotachi with [[Ame-no-Minakanushi|Amenominakanushi]] and regarded him as the primordial god of the [[Universe]].
He is a [[Hitorigami]], or a singular divinity born early in the universe.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-05-18 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Concepts of Kami : Hitorigami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110518151157/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-date=2011-05-18 |access-date=2023-03-22}}</ref>
He came after [[Amenotokotachi]] and before {{ill|Toyokumonu|ja|トヨクモノ}} {{Seven Generations of Kami}}
==See also== *[[Kamiyonanayo]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *Encyclopedia of Shinto - http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp *Kunitokotachi on the [https://www.historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kunitokotachi Japanese History Database].

{{short description|Shinto kami}} [[File:Kuninotokotachi.jpg|thumb]] In [[Shinto faith]], '''Kuninotokotachi''' {{Nihongo||国之常立神||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Kami'', in ''[[Kojiki]]''|}} {{Nihongo||国常立尊||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Mikoto'', in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (no-Mikoto here being an honorific of divinity|}} or '''Kuni-toko-tachi<ref name=":12">{{Cite book |last=Evans Lansing |first=Smith |title=The Complete Idiot's Guide to World Mythology |last2=Brown |first2=Nathan Robert |publisher=[[Alpha Books]] |year=2007 |isbn=9781592577644 |location=New York |pages=14}}</ref>''' is one of the two [[kami|God]]s born from "something like a [[Reed bed|reed]] that arose from the soil"<ref>[[Masaki Tsugita]], ''Kojiki'', 1977, {{ISBN|4-06-158207-0}}</ref> when the Earth was [[Chaos (cosmogony)|chaotic]]. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, he is the first of the [[Kamiyonanayo|seven generations of Divinities]] born after the [[Kotoamatsukami|first five divinities]] were born at the time of the creation of the Universe. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}, he is the first of the three divinities born after Heaven and Earth were born out of chaos, and is born from something looking like a reed-shoot growing between heaven and earth.<ref>''Nihongi - Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 (tr. from the original Chinese and Japanese by W.G. Aston, Charles E. Tuttle Cy. 1990)''</ref> He is known by mythology to reside on top of [[Mount Fuji]] (富士山).
Kuninotokotachi is described as a [[hitorigami]] and genderless in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, but is described as a male god in {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Shoki}}.
[[Yoshida Kanetomo]], the founder of the [[Yoshida Shintō]] sect, identified Kuninotokotachi with [[Ame-no-Minakanushi|Amenominakanushi]] and regarded him as the primordial god of the [[Universe]].
He is a [[Hitorigami]], or a singular divinity born early in the universe.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-05-18 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Concepts of Kami : Hitorigami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110518151157/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-date=2011-05-18 |access-date=2023-03-22}}</ref>
He came after [[Amenotokotachi]] and before {{ill|Toyokumonu|ja|トヨクモノ}} {{Seven Generations of Kami}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{short description|Shinto kami}} [[File:Kuninotokotachi.jpg|thumb]] In [[Shinto faith]], '''Kuninotokotachi''' {{Nihongo||国之常立神||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Kami'', in ''[[Kojiki]]''|}} {{Nihongo||国常立尊||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Mikoto'', in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (no-Mikoto here being an honorific of divinity|}} or '''Kuni-toko-tachi<ref name=":12">{{Cite book |last=Evans Lansing |first=Smith |title=The Complete Idiot's Guide to World Mythology |last2=Brown |first2=Nathan Robert |publisher=[[Alpha Books]] |year=2007 |isbn=9781592577644 |location=New York |pages=14}}</ref>''' is one of the two [[kami|God]]s born from "something like a [[Reed bed|reed]] that arose from the soil"<ref>[[Masaki Tsugita]], ''Kojiki'', 1977, {{ISBN|4-06-158207-0}}</ref> when the Earth was [[Chaos (cosmogony)|chaotic]]. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, he is the first of the [[Kamiyonanayo|seven generations of Divinities]] born after the [[Kotoamatsukami|first five divinities]] were born at the time of the creation of the Universe. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}, he is the first of the three divinities born after Heaven and Earth were born out of chaos, and is born from something looking like a reed-shoot growing between heaven and earth.<ref>''Nihongi - Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 (tr. from the original Chinese and Japanese by W.G. Aston, Charles E. Tuttle Cy. 1990)''</ref> He is known by mythology to reside on top of [[Mount Fuji]] (富士山).
Kuninotokotachi is described as a [[hitorigami]] and genderless in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, but is described as a male god in {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Shoki}}.
[[Yoshida Kanetomo]], the founder of the [[Yoshida Shintō]] sect, identified Kuninotokotachi with [[Ame-no-Minakanushi|Amenominakanushi]] and regarded him as the primordial god of the [[Universe]].
He is a [[Hitorigami]], or a singular divinity born early in the universe.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-05-18 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Concepts of Kami : Hitorigami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110518151157/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-date=2011-05-18 |access-date=2023-03-22}}</ref>
He came after [[Amenotokotachi]] and before {{ill|Toyokumonu|ja|トヨクモノ}} {{Seven Generations of Kami}}
==See also== *[[Kamiyonanayo]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *Encyclopedia of Shinto - http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp *Kunitokotachi on the [https://www.historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kunitokotachi Japanese History Database].

{{short description|Shinto kami}} [[File:Kuninotokotachi.jpg|thumb]] In [[Shinto faith]], '''Kuninotokotachi''' {{Nihongo||国之常立神||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Kami'', in ''[[Kojiki]]''|}} {{Nihongo||国常立尊||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Mikoto'', in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (no-Mikoto here being an honorific of divinity|}} or '''Kuni-toko-tachi<ref name=":12">{{Cite book |last=Evans Lansing |first=Smith |title=The Complete Idiot's Guide to World Mythology |last2=Brown |first2=Nathan Robert |publisher=[[Alpha Books]] |year=2007 |isbn=9781592577644 |location=New York |pages=14}}</ref>''' is one of the two [[kami|God]]s born from "something like a [[Reed bed|reed]] that arose from the soil"<ref>[[Masaki Tsugita]], ''Kojiki'', 1977, {{ISBN|4-06-158207-0}}</ref> when the Earth was [[Chaos (cosmogony)|chaotic]]. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, he is the first of the [[Kamiyonanayo|seven generations of Divinities]] born after the [[Kotoamatsukami|first five divinities]] were born at the time of the creation of the Universe. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}, he is the first of the three divinities born after Heaven and Earth were born out of chaos, and is born from something looking like a reed-shoot growing between heaven and earth.<ref>''Nihongi - Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 (tr. from the original Chinese and Japanese by W.G. Aston, Charles E. Tuttle Cy. 1990)''</ref> He is known by mythology to reside on top of [[Mount Fuji]] (富士山).
Kuninotokotachi is described as a [[hitorigami]] and genderless in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, but is described as a male god in {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Shoki}}.
[[Yoshida Kanetomo]], the founder of the [[Yoshida Shintō]] sect, identified Kuninotokotachi with [[Ame-no-Minakanushi|Amenominakanushi]] and regarded him as the primordial god of the [[Universe]].
He is a [[Hitorigami]], or a singular divinity born early in the universe.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-05-18 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Concepts of Kami : Hitorigami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110518151157/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-date=2011-05-18 |access-date=2023-03-22}}</ref>
He came after [[Amenotokotachi]] and before {{ill|Toyokumonu|ja|トヨクモノ}} {{Seven Generations of Kami}}

revision.text:
{{short description|Shinto kami}} [[File:Kuninotokotachi.jpg|thumb]] In [[Shinto faith]], '''Kuninotokotachi''' {{Nihongo||国之常立神||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Kami'', in ''[[Kojiki]]''|}} {{Nihongo||国常立尊||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Mikoto'', in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (no-Mikoto here being an honorific of divinity|}} or '''Kuni-toko-tachi<ref name=":12">{{Cite book |last=Evans Lansing |first=Smith |title=The Complete Idiot's Guide to World Mythology |last2=Brown |first2=Nathan Robert |publisher=[[Alpha Books]] |year=2007 |isbn=9781592577644 |location=New York |pages=14}}</ref>''' is one of the two [[kami|God]]s born from "something like a [[Reed bed|reed]] that arose from the soil"<ref>[[Masaki Tsugita]], ''Kojiki'', 1977, {{ISBN|4-06-158207-0}}</ref> when the Earth was [[Chaos (cosmogony)|chaotic]]. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, he is the first of the [[Kamiyonanayo|seven generations of Divinities]] born after the [[Kotoamatsukami|first five divinities]] were born at the time of the creation of the Universe. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}, he is the first of the three divinities born after Heaven and Earth were born out of chaos, and is born from something looking like a reed-shoot growing between heaven and earth.<ref>''Nihongi - Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 (tr. from the original Chinese and Japanese by W.G. Aston, Charles E. Tuttle Cy. 1990)''</ref> He is known by mythology to reside on top of [[Mount Fuji]] (富士山).
Kuninotokotachi is described as a [[hitorigami]] and genderless in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, but is described as a male god in {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Shoki}}.
[[Yoshida Kanetomo]], the founder of the [[Yoshida Shintō]] sect, identified Kuninotokotachi with [[Ame-no-Minakanushi|Amenominakanushi]] and regarded him as the primordial god of the [[Universe]].
He is a [[Hitorigami]], or a singular divinity born early in the universe.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-05-18 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Concepts of Kami : Hitorigami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110518151157/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-date=2011-05-18 |access-date=2023-03-22}}</ref>
He came after [[Amenotokotachi]] and before {{ill|Toyokumonu|ja|トヨクモノ}} {{Seven Generations of Kami}}
==See also== *[[Kamiyonanayo]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *Encyclopedia of Shinto - http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp *Kunitokotachi on the [https://www.historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kunitokotachi Japanese History Database].

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Bump-01-21]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{short description|Shinto kami}} [[File:Kuninotokotachi.jpg|thumb]] In [[Shinto faith]], '''Kuninotokotachi''' {{Nihongo||国之常立神||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Kami'', in ''[[Kojiki]]''|}} {{Nihongo||国常立尊||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Mikoto'', in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (no-Mikoto here being an honorific of divinity|}} or '''Kuni-toko-tachi<ref name=":12">{{Cite book |last=Evans Lansing |first=Smith |title=The Complete Idiot's Guide to World Mythology |last2=Brown |first2=Nathan Robert |publisher=[[Alpha Books]] |year=2007 |isbn=9781592577644 |location=New York |pages=14}}</ref>''' is one of the two [[kami|God]]s born from "something like a [[Reed bed|reed]] that arose from the soil"<ref>[[Masaki Tsugita]], ''Kojiki'', 1977, {{ISBN|4-06-158207-0}}</ref> when the Earth was [[Chaos (cosmogony)|chaotic]]. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, he is the first of the [[Kamiyonanayo|seven generations of Divinities]] born after the [[Kotoamatsukami|first five divinities]] were born at the time of the creation of the Universe. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}, he is the first of the three divinities born after Heaven and Earth were born out of chaos, and is born from something looking like a reed-shoot growing between heaven and earth.<ref>''Nihongi - Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 (tr. from the original Chinese and Japanese by W.G. Aston, Charles E. Tuttle Cy. 1990)''</ref> He is known by mythology to reside on top of [[Mount Fuji]] (富士山).
Kuninotokotachi is described as a [[hitorigami]] and genderless in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, but is described as a male god in {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Shoki}}.
[[Yoshida Kanetomo]], the founder of the [[Yoshida Shintō]] sect, identified Kuninotokotachi with [[Ame-no-Minakanushi|Amenominakanushi]] and regarded him as the primordial god of the [[Universe]].
He is a [[Hitorigami]], or a singular divinity born early in the universe.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-05-18 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Concepts of Kami : Hitorigami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110518151157/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-date=2011-05-18 |access-date=2023-03-22}}</ref>
He came after [[Amenotokotachi]] and before {{ill|Toyokumonu|ja|トヨクモノ}} {{Seven Generations of Kami}}
==See also== *[[Kamiyonanayo]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *Encyclopedia of Shinto - http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp *Kunitokotachi on the [https://www.historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kunitokotachi Japanese History Database].

Aston, Charles E. Tuttle Cy. 1990)''</ref> He is known by mythology to reside on top of [[Mount Fuji]] (富士山).

In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}, he is the first of the three divinities born after Heaven and Earth were born out of chaos, and is born from something looking like a reed-shoot growing between heaven and earth.<ref>''Nihongi - Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 (tr. from the original Chinese and Japanese by W.G. Aston, Charles E. Tuttle Cy. 1990)''</ref> He is known by mythology to reside on top of [[Mount Fuji]] (富士山).
Kuninotokotachi is described as a [[hitorigami]] and genderless in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, but is described as a male god in {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Shoki}}.
[[Yoshida Kanetomo]], the founder of the [[Yoshida Shintō]] sect, identified Kuninotokotachi with [[Ame-no-Minakanushi|Amenominakanushi]] and regarded him as the primordial god of the [[Universe]].
He is a [[Hitorigami]], or a singular divinity born early in the universe.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-05-18 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Concepts of Kami : Hitorigami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110518151157/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-date=2011-05-18 |access-date=2023-03-22}}</ref>
He came after [[Ame-no-Tokotachi]] and before {{ill|Toyokumonu|ja|トヨクモノ}} {{Seven Generations of Kami}}

revision.text:
{{short description|Shinto kami}} [[File:Kuninotokotachi.jpg|thumb]] In [[Shinto faith]], '''Kuninotokotachi''' {{Nihongo||国之常立神||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Kami'', in ''[[Kojiki]]''|}} {{Nihongo||国常立尊||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Mikoto'', in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (no-Mikoto here being an honorific of divinity|}} or '''Kuni-toko-tachi<ref name=":12">{{Cite book |last=Evans Lansing |first=Smith |title=The Complete Idiot's Guide to World Mythology |last2=Brown |first2=Nathan Robert |publisher=[[Alpha Books]] |year=2007 |isbn=9781592577644 |location=New York |pages=14}}</ref>''' is one of the two [[kami|God]]s born from "something like a [[Reed bed|reed]] that arose from the soil"<ref>[[Masaki Tsugita]], ''Kojiki'', 1977, {{ISBN|4-06-158207-0}}</ref> when the Earth was [[Chaos (cosmogony)|chaotic]]. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, he is the first of the [[Kamiyonanayo|seven generations of Divinities]] born after the [[Kotoamatsukami|first five divinities]] were born at the time of the creation of the Universe. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}, he is the first of the three divinities born after Heaven and Earth were born out of chaos, and is born from something looking like a reed-shoot growing between heaven and earth.<ref>''Nihongi - Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 (tr. from the original Chinese and Japanese by W.G. Aston, Charles E. Tuttle Cy. 1990)''</ref> He is known by mythology to reside on top of [[Mount Fuji]] (富士山).
Kuninotokotachi is described as a [[hitorigami]] and genderless in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, but is described as a male god in {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Shoki}}.
[[Yoshida Kanetomo]], the founder of the [[Yoshida Shintō]] sect, identified Kuninotokotachi with [[Ame-no-Minakanushi|Amenominakanushi]] and regarded him as the primordial god of the [[Universe]].
He is a [[Hitorigami]], or a singular divinity born early in the universe.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-05-18 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Concepts of Kami : Hitorigami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110518151157/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-date=2011-05-18 |access-date=2023-03-22}}</ref>
He came after [[Ame-no-Tokotachi]] and before {{ill|Toyokumonu|ja|トヨクモノ}} {{Seven Generations of Kami}}
==See also== *[[Kamiyonanayo]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *Encyclopedia of Shinto - http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp *Kunitokotachi on the [https://www.historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kunitokotachi Japanese History Database].

revision.text:
{{short description|Shinto kami}} [[File:Kuninotokotachi.jpg|thumb]] In [[Shinto faith]], '''Kuninotokotachi''' {{Nihongo||国之常立神||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Kami'', in ''[[Kojiki]]''|}} {{Nihongo||国常立尊||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Mikoto'', in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (no-Mikoto here being an honorific of divinity|}} or '''Kuni-toko-tachi<ref name=":12">{{Cite book |last=Evans Lansing |first=Smith |title=The Complete Idiot's Guide to World Mythology |last2=Brown |first2=Nathan Robert |publisher=[[Alpha Books]] |year=2007 |isbn=9781592577644 |location=New York |pages=14}}</ref>''' is one of the two [[kami|God]]s born from "something like a [[Reed bed|reed]] that arose from the soil"<ref>[[Masaki Tsugita]], ''Kojiki'', 1977, {{ISBN|4-06-158207-0}}</ref> when the Earth was [[Chaos (cosmogony)|chaotic]]. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, he is the first of the [[Kamiyonanayo|seven generations of Divinities]] born after the [[Kotoamatsukami|first five divinities]] were born at the time of the creation of the Universe. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}, he is the first of the three divinities born after Heaven and Earth were born out of chaos, and is born from something looking like a reed-shoot growing between heaven and earth.<ref>''Nihongi - Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 (tr. from the original Chinese and Japanese by W.G. Aston, Charles E. Tuttle Cy. 1990)''</ref> He is known by mythology to reside on top of [[Mount Fuji]] (富士山).
Kuninotokotachi is described as a [[hitorigami]] and genderless in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, but is described as a male god in {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Shoki}}.
[[Yoshida Kanetomo]], the founder of the [[Yoshida Shintō]] sect, identified Kuninotokotachi with [[Ame-no-Minakanushi|Amenominakanushi]] and regarded him as the primordial god of the [[Universe]].
He is a [[Hitorigami]], or a singular divinity born early in the universe.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-05-18 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Concepts of Kami : Hitorigami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110518151157/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-date=2011-05-18 |access-date=2023-03-22}}</ref>
He came after [[Ame-no-Tokotachi]] and before {{ill|Toyokumonu|ja|トヨクモノ|12=draft}} {{Seven Generations of Kami}}
==See also== *[[Kamiyonanayo]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *Encyclopedia of Shinto - http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp *Kunitokotachi on the [https://www.historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kunitokotachi Japanese History Database].
{{Japan-myth-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ca:Kunitokotatchi]][[commons:Category:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[es:Kunitokotachi]][[fr:Kunitokotachi]][[ja:国之常立神]][[mni:ꯀꯨꯅꯤ-ꯅꯣ-ꯇꯣꯀꯣꯇꯆꯤ]][[ru:Куни-но токотати-но микото]][[simple:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[th:คูนิ โนะ โทโกตาจิ]][[uk:Куні но токотаті но камі]][[zh:國常立尊]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q478504]] Wikidata name: Kuni-no-Tokotachi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Shinto kami}} [[File:Kuninotokotachi.jpg|thumb]] In [[:en:Shinto faith|Shinto faith]], '''Kuninotokotachi''' {{Nihongo||国之常立神||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Kami'', in ''[[Kojiki]]''|}} {{Nihongo||国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Mikoto'', in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (no-Mikoto here being an honorific of divinity|}} or '''Kuni-toko-tachi<ref name=":12">{{Cite book |last=Evans Lansing |first=Smith |title=The Complete Idiot's Guide to World Mythology |last2=Brown |first2=Nathan Robert |publisher=[[Alpha Books]] |year=2007 |isbn=9781592577644 |location=New York |pages=14}}</ref>''' is one of the two [[kami|God]]s born from "something like a [[:en:Reed bed|reed]] that arose from the soil"<ref>[[Masaki Tsugita]], ''Kojiki'', 1977, {{ISBN|4-06-158207-0}}</ref> when the Earth was [[:en:Chaos (cosmogony)|chaotic]]. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, he is the first of the [[Kamiyonanayo|seven generations of Divinities]] born after the [[Kotoamatsukami|first five divinities]] were born at the time of the creation of the Universe. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}, he is the first of the three divinities born after Heaven and Earth were born out of chaos, and is born from something looking like a reed-shoot growing between heaven and earth.<ref>''Nihongi - Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 (tr. from the original Chinese and Japanese by W.G. Aston, Charles E. Tuttle Cy. 1990)''</ref> He is known by mythology to reside on top of [[:en:Mount Fuji|Mount Fuji]] (富士山).
Kuninotokotachi is described as a [[:en:Hitorigami|Hitorigami]] and genderless in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, but is described as a male god in {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Shoki}}.
[[Yoshida Kanetomo]], the founder of the [[:en:Yoshida Shintō|Yoshida Shintō]] sect, identified Kuninotokotachi with [[Ame-no-Minakanushi|Amenominakanushi]] and regarded him as the primordial god of the [[:en:Universe|Universe]].
He is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Hitorigami]], or a singular divinity born early in the universe.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-05-18 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Concepts of Kami : Hitorigami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110518151157/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-date=2011-05-18 |access-date=2023-03-22}}</ref>
He came after [[Ame-no-Tokotachi]] and before {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumonu|ja|トヨクモノ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu|qq=}} {{Seven Generations of Kami}}
==See also== *[[Kamiyonanayo]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *Encyclopedia of Shinto - http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp *Kunitokotachi on the [https://www.historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kunitokotachi Japanese History Database].

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[d:" to "[[da:d:"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ca:Kunitokotatchi]][[sn:commons:Category:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[es:Kunitokotachi]][[fr:Kunitokotachi]][[ja:国之常立神]][[mni:ꯀꯨꯅꯤ-ꯅꯣ-ꯇꯣꯀꯣꯇꯆꯤ]][[ru:Куни-но токотати-но микото]][[simple:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[th:คูนิ โนะ โทโกตาจิ]][[uk:Куні но токотаті но камі]][[zh:國常立尊]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q478504]] Wikidata name: Kuni-no-Tokotachi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Shinto kami}} [[File:Kuninotokotachi.jpg|thumb]] In [[:en:Shinto faith|Shinto faith]], '''Kuninotokotachi''' {{Nihongo||国之常立神||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Kami'', in ''[[Kojiki]]''|}} {{Nihongo||国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Mikoto'', in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (no-Mikoto here being an honorific of divinity|}} or '''Kuni-toko-tachi<ref name=":12">{{Cite book |last=Evans Lansing |first=Smith |title=The Complete Idiot's Guide to World Mythology |last2=Brown |first2=Nathan Robert |publisher=[[Alpha Books]] |year=2007 |isbn=9781592577644 |location=New York |pages=14}}</ref>''' is one of the two [[kami|God]]s born from "something like a [[:en:Reed bed|reed]] that arose from the soil"<ref>[[Masaki Tsugita]], ''Kojiki'', 1977, {{ISBN|4-06-158207-0}}</ref> when the Earth was [[:en:Chaos (cosmogony)|chaotic]]. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, he is the first of the [[Kamiyonanayo|seven generations of Divinities]] born after the [[Kotoamatsukami|first five divinities]] were born at the time of the creation of the Universe. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}, he is the first of the three divinities born after Heaven and Earth were born out of chaos, and is born from something looking like a reed-shoot growing between heaven and earth.<ref>''Nihongi - Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 (tr. from the original Chinese and Japanese by W.G. Aston, Charles E. Tuttle Cy. 1990)''</ref> He is known by mythology to reside on top of [[:en:Mount Fuji|Mount Fuji]] (富士山).
Kuninotokotachi is described as a [[:en:Hitorigami|Hitorigami]] and genderless in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, but is described as a male god in {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Shoki}}.
[[Yoshida Kanetomo]], the founder of the [[:en:Yoshida Shintō|Yoshida Shintō]] sect, identified Kuninotokotachi with [[Ame-no-Minakanushi|Amenominakanushi]] and regarded him as the primordial god of the [[:en:Universe|Universe]].
He is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Hitorigami]], or a singular divinity born early in the universe.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-05-18 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Concepts of Kami : Hitorigami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110518151157/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-date=2011-05-18 |access-date=2023-03-22}}</ref>
He came after [[Ame-no-Tokotachi]] and before {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumonu|ja|トヨクモノ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu|qq=}} {{Seven Generations of Kami}}
==See also== *[[Kamiyonanayo]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *Encyclopedia of Shinto - http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp *Kunitokotachi on the [https://www.historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kunitokotachi Japanese History Database].
{{Japan-myth-stub}}

Aston, Charles E. Tuttle Cy. 1990)''</ref> He is known by mythology to reside on top of [[:en:Mount Fuji|Mount Fuji]] (富士山).
Kuninotokotachi is described as a [[:en:Hitorigami|Hitorigami]] and genderless in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, but is described as a male god in {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Shoki}}.

He is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Hitorigami]], or a singular divinity born early in the universe.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-05-18 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Concepts of Kami : Hitorigami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110518151157/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-date=2011-05-18 |access-date=2023-03-22}}</ref>
He came after [[Ame-no-Tokotachi]] and before {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumonu|ja|トヨクモノ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu|qq=}} {{Seven Generations of Kami}}

[[en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]]
[[Category:Pages without translation templates]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ca:Kunitokotatchi]] {{Commons category|Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} [[es:Kunitokotachi]] [[fr:Kunitokotachi]] [[ja:国之常立神]] [[mni:ꯀꯨꯅꯤ-ꯅꯣ-ꯇꯣꯀꯣꯇꯆꯤ]] [[ru:Куни-но токотати-но микото]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] [[th:คูนิ โนะ โทโกตาจิ]] [[uk:Куні но токотаті но камі]] [[zh:國常立尊]]
{{wikidata link|Q478504}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ca:Kunitokotatchi]][[sn:commons:Category:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[es:Kunitokotachi]][[fr:Kunitokotachi]][[ja:国之常立神]][[mni:ꯀꯨꯅꯤ-ꯅꯣ-ꯇꯣꯀꯣꯇꯆꯤ]][[ru:Куни-но токотати-но микото]][[simple:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[th:คูนิ โนะ โทโกตาจิ]][[uk:Куні но токотаті но камі]][[zh:國常立尊]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q478504]] Wikidata name: Kuni-no-Tokotachi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Shinto kami}} [[File:Kuninotokotachi.jpg|thumb]] In [[:en:Shinto faith|Shinto faith]], '''Kuninotokotachi''' {{Nihongo||国之常立神||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Kami'', in ''[[Kojiki]]''|}} {{Nihongo||国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Mikoto'', in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (no-Mikoto here being an honorific of divinity|}} or '''Kuni-toko-tachi<ref name=":12">{{Cite book |last=Evans Lansing |first=Smith |title=The Complete Idiot's Guide to World Mythology |last2=Brown |first2=Nathan Robert |publisher=[[Alpha Books]] |year=2007 |isbn=9781592577644 |location=New York |pages=14}}</ref>''' is one of the two [[kami|God]]s born from "something like a [[:en:Reed bed|reed]] that arose from the soil"<ref>[[Masaki Tsugita]], ''Kojiki'', 1977, {{ISBN|4-06-158207-0}}</ref> when the Earth was [[:en:Chaos (cosmogony)|chaotic]]. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, he is the first of the [[Kamiyonanayo|seven generations of Divinities]] born after the [[Kotoamatsukami|first five divinities]] were born at the time of the creation of the Universe. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}, he is the first of the three divinities born after Heaven and Earth were born out of chaos, and is born from something looking like a reed-shoot growing between heaven and earth.<ref>''Nihongi - Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 (tr. from the original Chinese and Japanese by W.G. Aston, Charles E. Tuttle Cy. 1990)''</ref> He is known by mythology to reside on top of [[:en:Mount Fuji|Mount Fuji]] (富士山).
Kuninotokotachi is described as a [[:en:Hitorigami|Hitorigami]] and genderless in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, but is described as a male god in {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Shoki}}.
[[Yoshida Kanetomo]], the founder of the [[:en:Yoshida Shintō|Yoshida Shintō]] sect, identified Kuninotokotachi with [[Ame-no-Minakanushi|Amenominakanushi]] and regarded him as the primordial god of the [[:en:Universe|Universe]].
He is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Hitorigami]], or a singular divinity born early in the universe.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-05-18 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Concepts of Kami : Hitorigami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110518151157/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-date=2011-05-18 |access-date=2023-03-22}}</ref>
He came after [[Ame-no-Tokotachi]] and before {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumonu|ja|トヨクモノ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu|qq=}} {{Seven Generations of Kami}}
==See also== *[[Kamiyonanayo]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *Encyclopedia of Shinto - http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp *Kunitokotachi on the [https://www.historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kunitokotachi Japanese History Database].
{{Japan-myth-stub}}

In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, he is the first of the [[Kamiyonanayo|seven generations of Divinities]] born after the [[Kotoamatsukami|first five divinities]] were born at the time of the creation of the Universe. Aston, Charles E. Tuttle Cy. 1990)''</ref> He is known by mythology to reside on top of [[:en:Mount Fuji|Mount Fuji]] (富士山).
Kuninotokotachi is described as a [[:en:Hitorigami|Hitorigami]] and genderless in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, but is described as a male god in {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Shoki}}.
[[Yoshida Kanetomo]], the founder of the [[:en:Yoshida Shintō|Yoshida Shintō]] sect, identified Kuninotokotachi with [[Ame-no-Minakanushi|Amenominakanushi]] and regarded him as the primordial god of the [[:en:Universe|Universe]].
He is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Hitorigami]], or a singular divinity born early in the universe.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-05-18 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Concepts of Kami : Hitorigami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110518151157/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-date=2011-05-18 |access-date=2023-03-22}}</ref>
He came after [[Ame-no-Tokotachi]] and before {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumonu|ja|トヨクモノ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu|qq=}} {{Seven Generations of Kami}}

In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, he is the first of the [[Kamiyonanayo|seven generations of Divinities]] born after the [[Kotoamatsukami|first five divinities]] were born at the time of the creation of the Universe. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}, he is the first of the three divinities born after Heaven and Earth were born out of chaos, and is born from something looking like a reed-shoot growing between heaven and earth.<ref>''Nihongi - Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 (tr. from the original Chinese and Japanese by W.G. Aston, Charles E. Tuttle Cy. 1990)''</ref> He is known by mythology to reside on top of [[:en:Mount Fuji|Mount Fuji]] (富士山).
Kuninotokotachi is described as a [[:en:Hitorigami|Hitorigami]] and genderless in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, but is described as a male god in {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Shoki}}.
[[Yoshida Kanetomo]], the founder of the [[:en:Yoshida Shintō|Yoshida Shintō]] sect, identified Kuninotokotachi with [[Ame-no-Minakanushi|Amenominakanushi]] and regarded him as the primordial god of the [[:en:Universe|Universe]].
He is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Hitorigami]], or a singular divinity born early in the universe.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-05-18 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Concepts of Kami : Hitorigami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110518151157/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-date=2011-05-18 |access-date=2023-03-22}}</ref>
He came after [[Ame-no-Tokotachi]] and before {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumonu|ja|トヨクモノ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu|qq=}} {{Seven Generations of Kami}}
==See also== *[[Kamiyonanayo]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *Encyclopedia of Shinto - http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp *Kunitokotachi on the [https://www.historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kunitokotachi Japanese History Database].
{{Japan-myth-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ca:Kunitokotatchi]][[commons:Category:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[es:Kunitokotachi]][[fr:Kunitokotachi]][[ja:国之常立神]][[mni:ꯀꯨꯅꯤ-ꯅꯣ-ꯇꯣꯀꯣꯇꯆꯤ]][[ru:Куни-но токотати-но микото]][[simple:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[th:คูนิ โนะ โทโกตาจิ]][[uk:Куні но токотаті но камі]][[zh:國常立尊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ca:Kunitokotatchi]][[commons:Category:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[es:Kunitokotachi]][[fr:Kunitokotachi]][[ja:国之常立神]][[mni:ꯀꯨꯅꯤ-ꯅꯣ-ꯇꯣꯀꯣꯇꯆꯤ]][[ru:Куни-но токотати-но микото]][[simple:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[th:คูนิ โนะ โทโกตาจิ]][[uk:Куні но токотаті но камі]][[zh:國常立尊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ca:Kunitokotatchi]][[sn:commons:Category:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[es:Kunitokotachi]][[fr:Kunitokotachi]][[ja:国之常立神]][[mni:ꯀꯨꯅꯤ-ꯅꯣ-ꯇꯣꯀꯣꯇꯆꯤ]][[ru:Куни-но токотати-но микото]][[simple:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[th:คูนิ โนะ โทโกตาจิ]][[uk:Куні но токотаті но камі]][[zh:國常立尊]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q478504]] Wikidata name: Kuni-no-Tokotachi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Shinto kami}} [[File:Kuninotokotachi.jpg|thumb]] In [[:en:Shinto faith|Shinto faith]], '''Kuninotokotachi''' {{Nihongo||国之常立神||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Kami'', in ''[[Kojiki]]''|}} {{Nihongo||国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Mikoto'', in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (no-Mikoto here being an honorific of divinity|}} or '''Kuni-toko-tachi<ref name=":12">{{Cite book |last=Evans Lansing |first=Smith |title=The Complete Idiot's Guide to World Mythology |last2=Brown |first2=Nathan Robert |publisher=[[Alpha Books]] |year=2007 |isbn=9781592577644 |location=New York |pages=14}}</ref>''' is one of the two [[kami|God]]s born from "something like a [[:en:Reed bed|reed]] that arose from the soil"<ref>[[Masaki Tsugita]], ''Kojiki'', 1977, {{ISBN|4-06-158207-0}}</ref> when the Earth was [[:en:Chaos (cosmogony)|chaotic]]. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, he is the first of the [[Kamiyonanayo|seven generations of Divinities]] born after the [[Kotoamatsukami|first five divinities]] were born at the time of the creation of the Universe. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}, he is the first of the three divinities born after Heaven and Earth were born out of chaos, and is born from something looking like a reed-shoot growing between heaven and earth.<ref>''Nihongi - Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 (tr. from the original Chinese and Japanese by W.G. Aston, Charles E. Tuttle Cy. 1990)''</ref> He is known by mythology to reside on top of [[:en:Mount Fuji|Mount Fuji]] (富士山).
Kuninotokotachi is described as a [[:en:Hitorigami|Hitorigami]] and genderless in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, but is described as a male god in {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Shoki}}.
[[Yoshida Kanetomo]], the founder of the [[:en:Yoshida Shintō|Yoshida Shintō]] sect, identified Kuninotokotachi with [[Ame-no-Minakanushi|Amenominakanushi]] and regarded him as the primordial god of the [[:en:Universe|Universe]].
He is a [[:en:Hitorigami|Hitorigami]], or a singular divinity born early in the universe.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-05-18 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Concepts of Kami : Hitorigami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110518151157/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-date=2011-05-18 |access-date=2023-03-22}}</ref>
He came after [[Ame-no-Tokotachi]] and before {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumonu|ja|トヨクモノ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu|qq=}} {{Seven Generations of Kami}}
==See also== *[[Kamiyonanayo]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *Encyclopedia of Shinto - http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp *Kunitokotachi on the [https://www.historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kunitokotachi Japanese History Database].
{{Japan-myth-stub}}

микото]][[simple:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[th:คูนิ โนะ โทโกตาจิ]][[uk:Куні но токотаті но камі]][[zh:國常立尊]] <!--interwikis from микото]][[simple:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[th:คูนิ โนะ โทโกตาจิ]][[uk:Куні но токотаті но камі]][[zh:國常立尊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ca:Kunitokotatchi]][[sn:commons:Category:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[es:Kunitokotachi]][[fr:Kunitokotachi]][[ja:国之常立神]][[mni:ꯀꯨꯅꯤ-ꯅꯣ-ꯇꯣꯀꯣꯇꯆꯤ]][[ru:Куни-но токотати-но микото]][[simple:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[th:คูนิ โนะ โทโกตาจิ]][[uk:Куні но токотаті но камі]][[zh:國常立尊]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q478504]] Wikidata name: Kuni-no-Tokotachi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Shinto kami}} [[File:Kuninotokotachi.jpg|thumb]] In [[:en:Shinto faith|Shinto faith]], '''Kuninotokotachi''' {{Nihongo||国之常立神||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Kami'', in ''[[Kojiki]]''|}} {{Nihongo||国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Mikoto'', in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (no-Mikoto here being an honorific of divinity|}} or '''Kuni-toko-tachi<ref name=":12">{{Cite book |last=Evans Lansing |first=Smith |title=The Complete Idiot's Guide to World Mythology |last2=Brown |first2=Nathan Robert |publisher=[[Alpha Books]] |year=2007 |isbn=9781592577644 |location=New York |pages=14}}</ref>''' is one of the two [[kami|God]]s born from "something like a {{ill|Reed bed|en|Reed bed|WD=Q620424|lt=reed}} that arose from the soil"<ref>[[Masaki Tsugita]], ''Kojiki'', 1977, {{ISBN|4-06-158207-0}}</ref> when the Earth was {{ill|Chaos (cosmogony)|en|Chaos (cosmogony)|WD=Q17508277|lt=chaotic}}. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, he is the first of the [[Kamiyonanayo|seven generations of Divinities]] born after the [[Kotoamatsukami|first five divinities]] were born at the time of the creation of the Universe. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}, he is the first of the three divinities born after Heaven and Earth were born out of chaos, and is born from something looking like a reed-shoot growing between heaven and earth.<ref>''Nihongi - Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 (tr. from the original Chinese and Japanese by W.G. Aston, Charles E. Tuttle Cy. 1990)''</ref> He is known by mythology to reside on top of {{ill|Mount Fuji|en|Mount Fuji|WD=Q39231|lt=Mount Fuji}} (富士山).
Kuninotokotachi is described as a {{ill|Hitorigami|en|Hitorigami|WD=Q3138614|lt=Hitorigami}} and genderless in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, but is described as a male god in {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Shoki}}.
[[Yoshida Kanetomo]], the founder of the {{ill|Yoshida Shintō|en|Yoshida Shintō|WD=Q1760351|lt=Yoshida Shintō}} sect, identified Kuninotokotachi with [[Ame-no-Minakanushi|Amenominakanushi]] and regarded him as the primordial god of the {{ill|Universe|en|Universe|WD=Q1|lt=Universe}}.
He is a {{ill|Hitorigami|en|Hitorigami|WD=Q3138614|lt=Hitorigami}}, or a singular divinity born early in the universe.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-05-18 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Concepts of Kami : Hitorigami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110518151157/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-date=2011-05-18 |access-date=2023-03-22}}</ref>
He came after [[Ame-no-Tokotachi]] and before {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumonu|ja|トヨクモノ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu|qq=}} {{Seven Generations of Kami}}
==See also== *[[Kamiyonanayo]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *Encyclopedia of Shinto - http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp *Kunitokotachi on the [https://www.historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kunitokotachi Japanese History Database].
{{Japan-myth-stub}}

In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, he is the first of the [[Kamiyonanayo|seven generations of Divinities]] born after the [[Kotoamatsukami|first five divinities]] were born at the time of the creation of the Universe. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}, he is the first of the three divinities born after Heaven and Earth were born out of chaos, and is born from something looking like a reed-shoot growing between heaven and earth.<ref>''Nihongi - Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 (tr. from the original Chinese and Japanese by W.G. Aston, Charles E. Tuttle Cy. 1990)''</ref> He is known by mythology to reside on top of {{ill|Mount Fuji|en|Mount Fuji|WD=Q39231|lt=Mount Fuji}} (富士山).
Kuninotokotachi is described as a {{ill|Hitorigami|en|Hitorigami|WD=Q3138614|lt=Hitorigami}} and genderless in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, but is described as a male god in {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Shoki}}.
[[Yoshida Kanetomo]], the founder of the {{ill|Yoshida Shintō|en|Yoshida Shintō|WD=Q1760351|lt=Yoshida Shintō}} sect, identified Kuninotokotachi with [[Ame-no-Minakanushi|Amenominakanushi]] and regarded him as the primordial god of the {{ill|Universe|en|Universe|WD=Q1|lt=Universe}}.
He is a {{ill|Hitorigami|en|Hitorigami|WD=Q3138614|lt=Hitorigami}}, or a singular divinity born early in the universe.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-05-18 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Concepts of Kami : Hitorigami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110518151157/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-date=2011-05-18 |access-date=2023-03-22}}</ref>
He came after [[Ame-no-Tokotachi]] and before {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumonu|ja|トヨクモノ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu|qq=}} {{Seven Generations of Kami}}
==See also== *[[Kamiyonanayo]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *Encyclopedia of Shinto - http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp *Kunitokotachi on the [https://www.historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kunitokotachi Japanese History Database].
{{Japan-myth-stub}}

In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, he is the first of the [[Kamiyonanayo|seven generations of Divinities]] born after the [[Kotoamatsukami|first five divinities]] were born at the time of the creation of the Universe. Aston, Charles E. Tuttle Cy. 1990)''</ref> He is known by mythology to reside on top of {{ill|Mount Fuji|en|Mount Fuji|WD=Q39231|lt=Mount Fuji}} (富士山).

[[Yoshida Kanetomo]], the founder of the {{ill|Yoshida Shintō|en|Yoshida Shintō|WD=Q1760351|lt=Yoshida Shintō}} sect, identified Kuninotokotachi with [[Ame-no-Minakanushi|Amenominakanushi]] and regarded him as the primordial god of the {{ill|Universe|en|Universe|WD=Q1|lt=Universe}}.
He is a {{ill|Hitorigami|en|Hitorigami|WD=Q3138614|lt=Hitorigami}}, or a singular divinity born early in the universe.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-05-18 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Concepts of Kami : Hitorigami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110518151157/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-date=2011-05-18 |access-date=2023-03-22}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ca:Kunitokotatchi]][[co:commons:Category:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[es:Kunitokotachi]][[fr:Kunitokotachi]][[ja:国之常立神]][[mni:ꯀꯨꯅꯤ-ꯅꯣ-ꯇꯣꯀꯣꯇꯆꯤ]][[ru:Куни-но токотати-но микото]][[simple:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[th:คูนิ โนะ โทโกตาจิ]][[uk:Куні но токотаті но камі]][[zh:國常立尊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ca:Kunitokotatchi]][[co:commons:Category:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[es:Kunitokotachi]][[fr:Kunitokotachi]][[ja:国之常立神]][[mni:ꯀꯨꯅꯤ-ꯅꯣ-ꯇꯣꯀꯣꯇꯆꯤ]][[ru:Куни-но токотати-но микото]][[simple:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[th:คูนิ โนะ โทโกตาจิ]][[uk:Куні но токотаті но камі]][[zh:國常立尊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ca:Kunitokotatchi]][[sn:commons:Category:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[es:Kunitokotachi]][[fr:Kunitokotachi]][[ja:国之常立神]][[mni:ꯀꯨꯅꯤ-ꯅꯣ-ꯇꯣꯀꯣꯇꯆꯤ]][[ru:Куни-но токотати-но микото]][[simple:Kuni-no-Tokotachi]][[th:คูนิ โนะ โทโกตาจิ]][[uk:Куні но токотаті но камі]][[zh:國常立尊]]
{{short description|Shinto kami}} [[File:Kuninotokotachi.jpg|thumb]] In [[:en:Shinto faith|Shinto faith]], '''Kuninotokotachi''' {{Nihongo||国之常立神||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Kami'', in ''[[Kojiki]]''|}} {{Nihongo||国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Mikoto'', in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (no-Mikoto here being an honorific of divinity|}} or '''Kuni-toko-tachi<ref name=":12">{{Cite book |last=Evans Lansing |first=Smith |title=The Complete Idiot's Guide to World Mythology |last2=Brown |first2=Nathan Robert |publisher=[[Alpha Books]] |year=2007 |isbn=9781592577644 |location=New York |pages=14}}</ref>''' is one of the two [[kami|God]]s born from "something like a {{ill|Reed bed|en|Reed bed|WD=Q620424|lt=reed|1=Reed bed}} that arose from the soil"<ref>[[Masaki Tsugita]], ''Kojiki'', 1977, {{ISBN|4-06-158207-0}}</ref> when the Earth was {{ill|Chaos (cosmogony)|en|Chaos (cosmogony)|WD=Q17508277|lt=chaotic|1=Chaos (cosmogony)}}. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, he is the first of the [[Kamiyonanayo|seven generations of Divinities]] born after the [[Kotoamatsukami|first five divinities]] were born at the time of the creation of the Universe. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}, he is the first of the three divinities born after Heaven and Earth were born out of chaos, and is born from something looking like a reed-shoot growing between heaven and earth.<ref>''Nihongi - Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 (tr. from the original Chinese and Japanese by W.G. Aston, Charles E. Tuttle Cy. 1990)''</ref> He is known by mythology to reside on top of {{ill|Mount Fuji|en|Mount Fuji|WD=Q39231|lt=Mount Fuji|1=Mount Fuji}} (富士山).
Kuninotokotachi is described as a {{ill|Hitorigami|en|Hitorigami|WD=Q3138614|lt=Hitorigami|1=Hitorigami}} and genderless in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, but is described as a male god in {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Shoki}}.
[[Yoshida Kanetomo]], the founder of the {{ill|Yoshida Shintō|en|Yoshida Shintō|WD=Q1760351|lt=Yoshida Shintō|1=Yoshida Shintō}} sect, identified Kuninotokotachi with [[Ame-no-Minakanushi|Amenominakanushi]] and regarded him as the primordial god of the {{ill|Universe|en|Universe|WD=Q1|lt=Universe|1=Universe}}.
He is a {{ill|Hitorigami|en|Hitorigami|WD=Q3138614|lt=Hitorigami|1=Hitorigami}}, or a singular divinity born early in the universe.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-05-18 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Concepts of Kami : Hitorigami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110518151157/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-date=2011-05-18 |access-date=2023-03-22}}</ref>
He came after [[Ame-no-Tokotachi]] and before {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumonu|ja|トヨクモノ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu|qq=}} {{Seven Generations of Kami}}
==See also== *[[Kamiyonanayo]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *Encyclopedia of Shinto - http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp *Kunitokotachi on the [https://www.historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kunitokotachi Japanese History Database].
{{Japan-myth-stub}}

He is a {{ill|Hitorigami|en|Hitorigami|WD=Q3138614|lt=Hitorigami|1=Hitorigami}}, or a singular divinity born early in the universe.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-05-18 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Concepts of Kami : Hitorigami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110518151157/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-date=2011-05-18 |access-date=2023-03-22}}</ref>
He came after [[Ame-no-Tokotachi]] and before {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumonu|ja|トヨクモノ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu|qq=}} {{Seven Generations of Kami}}
==See also== *[[Kamiyonanayo]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *Encyclopedia of Shinto - http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp *Kunitokotachi on the [https://www.historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kunitokotachi Japanese History Database].

{{short description|Shinto kami}} [[File:Kuninotokotachi.jpg|thumb]] In [[:en:Shinto faith|Shinto faith]], '''Kuninotokotachi''' {{Nihongo||国之常立神||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Kami'', in ''[[Kojiki]]''|}} {{Nihongo||国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Mikoto'', in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (no-Mikoto here being an honorific of divinity|}} or '''Kuni-toko-tachi<ref name=":12">{{Cite book |last=Evans Lansing |first=Smith |title=The Complete Idiot's Guide to World Mythology |last2=Brown |first2=Nathan Robert |publisher=[[Alpha Books]] |year=2007 |isbn=9781592577644 |location=New York |pages=14}}</ref>''' is one of the two [[kami|God]]s born from "something like a {{ill|Reed bed|en|Reed bed|WD=Q620424|lt=reed|1=Reed bed}} that arose from the soil"<ref>[[Masaki Tsugita]], ''Kojiki'', 1977, {{ISBN|4-06-158207-0}}</ref> when the Earth was {{ill|Chaos (cosmogony)|en|Chaos (cosmogony)|WD=Q17508277|lt=chaotic|1=Chaos (cosmogony)}}. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, he is the first of the [[Kamiyonanayo|seven generations of Divinities]] born after the [[Kotoamatsukami|first five divinities]] were born at the time of the creation of the Universe. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}, he is the first of the three divinities born after Heaven and Earth were born out of chaos, and is born from something looking like a reed-shoot growing between heaven and earth.<ref>''Nihongi - Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 (tr. from the original Chinese and Japanese by W.G. Aston, Charles E. Tuttle Cy. 1990)''</ref> He is known by mythology to reside on top of {{ill|Mount Fuji|en|Mount Fuji|WD=Q39231|lt=Mount Fuji|1=Mount Fuji}} (富士山).
Kuninotokotachi is described as a {{ill|Hitorigami|en|Hitorigami|WD=Q3138614|lt=Hitorigami|1=Hitorigami}} and genderless in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, but is described as a male god in {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Shoki}}.
[[Yoshida Kanetomo]], the founder of the {{ill|Yoshida Shintō|en|Yoshida Shintō|WD=Q1760351|lt=Yoshida Shintō|1=Yoshida Shintō}} sect, identified Kuninotokotachi with [[Ame-no-Minakanushi|Amenominakanushi]] and regarded him as the primordial god of the {{ill|Universe|en|Universe|WD=Q1|lt=Universe|1=Universe}}.
He is a {{ill|Hitorigami|en|Hitorigami|WD=Q3138614|lt=Hitorigami|1=Hitorigami}}, or a singular divinity born early in the universe.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-05-18 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Concepts of Kami : Hitorigami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110518151157/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-date=2011-05-18 |access-date=2023-03-22}}</ref>
He came after [[Ame-no-Tokotachi]] and before {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumonu|ja|トヨクモノ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu|qq=}} {{Seven Generations of Kami}}
==See also== *[[Kamiyonanayo]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *Encyclopedia of Shinto - http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp *Kunitokotachi on the [https://www.historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kunitokotachi Japanese History Database].

{{short description|Shinto kami}} [[File:Kuninotokotachi.jpg|thumb]] In [[:en:Shinto faith|Shinto faith]], '''Kuninotokotachi''' {{Nihongo||国之常立神||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Kami'', in ''[[Kojiki]]''|}} {{Nihongo||国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神||extra=''Kuninotokotachi-no-Mikoto'', in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (no-Mikoto here being an honorific of divinity|}} or '''Kuni-toko-tachi<ref name=":12">{{Cite book |last=Evans Lansing |first=Smith |title=The Complete Idiot's Guide to World Mythology |last2=Brown |first2=Nathan Robert |publisher=[[Alpha Books]] |year=2007 |isbn=9781592577644 |location=New York |pages=14}}</ref>''' is one of the two [[kami|God]]s born from "something like a {{ill|Reed bed|en|Reed bed|qid=Q620424|lt=reed|1=Reed bed}} that arose from the soil"<ref>[[Masaki Tsugita]], ''Kojiki'', 1977, {{ISBN|4-06-158207-0}}</ref> when the Earth was {{ill|Chaos (cosmogony)|en|Chaos (cosmogony)|qid=Q17508277|lt=chaotic|1=Chaos (cosmogony)}}. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, he is the first of the [[Kamiyonanayo|seven generations of Divinities]] born after the [[Kotoamatsukami|first five divinities]] were born at the time of the creation of the Universe. In the {{lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}, he is the first of the three divinities born after Heaven and Earth were born out of chaos, and is born from something looking like a reed-shoot growing between heaven and earth.<ref>''Nihongi - Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 (tr. from the original Chinese and Japanese by W.G. Aston, Charles E. Tuttle Cy. 1990)''</ref> He is known by mythology to reside on top of {{ill|Mount Fuji|en|Mount Fuji|qid=Q39231|lt=Mount Fuji|1=Mount Fuji}} (富士山).
Kuninotokotachi is described as a {{ill|Hitorigami|en|Hitorigami|qid=Q3138614|lt=Hitorigami|1=Hitorigami}} and genderless in {{lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}, but is described as a male god in {{lang|ja-latn|Nihon Shoki}}.
[[Yoshida Kanetomo]], the founder of the {{ill|Yoshida Shintō|en|Yoshida Shintō|qid=Q1760351|lt=Yoshida Shintō|1=Yoshida Shintō}} sect, identified Kuninotokotachi with [[Ame-no-Minakanushi|Amenominakanushi]] and regarded him as the primordial god of the {{ill|Universe|en|Universe|qid=Q1|lt=Universe|1=Universe}}.
He is a {{ill|Hitorigami|en|Hitorigami|qid=Q3138614|lt=Hitorigami|1=Hitorigami}}, or a singular divinity born early in the universe.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-05-18 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Concepts of Kami : Hitorigami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110518151157/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=10 |archive-date=2011-05-18 |access-date=2023-03-22}}</ref>
He came after [[Ame-no-Tokotachi]] and before {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumonu|ja|トヨクモノ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu|qq=}} {{Seven Generations of Kami}}
==See also== *[[Kamiyonanayo]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *Encyclopedia of Shinto - http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp *Kunitokotachi on the [https://www.historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kunitokotachi Japanese History Database].
{{Japan-myth-stub}}

Aston, Charles E. Tuttle Cy. 1990)''</ref> He is known by mythology to reside on top of {{ill|Mount Fuji|en|Mount Fuji|qid=Q39231|lt=Mount Fuji|1=Mount Fuji}} (富士山).
